articles.headline: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi returns to coach national team
articles.description: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi has been hired as national team coach for the second time. He was appointed to replace Mircea Lucescu, who died two weeks ago.

images.name: Gheorghe Hagi Romania coach_260421 [1024x576]
images.caption: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.
images.alt: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.

articles.headline: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
articles.description: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

images.name: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
images.caption: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

articles.headline: Ireland to fulfil Nations League games with Israel despite call for ban
articles.description: The Republic of Ireland said they will fulfil their Nations League fixtures against Israel in the autumn despite calling for the country to be banned by UEFA last year.

images.name: Ireland football logo [1296x729]
images.caption: The Irish football federation will call on UEFA to ban Israel.
images.alt: he official badge of the Republic of Ireland national football team on a shirt during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Group F match between France and Jamaica at Sydney Football Stadium on July 23, 2023 in Sydney, Australia

articles.headline: Nations League draw: England get Spain, Croatia, Czechia
articles.description: England face a rematch with their Euro 2024 final conquerors Spain in the 2026-27 Nations League.

images.name: England vs Spain Euro 2024 final [1296x729]
images.caption: Jude Bellingham and England were beaten by Spain the final of Euro 2024.
images.alt: Jude Bellingham of England in action during the UEFA EURO 2024 final match between Spain and England at Olympiastadion on July 14, 2024 in Berlin, Germany.

images.name: Did Thomas Tuchel deserve a contract extension with England?
images.caption: ESPN's Nedum Onuoha and Julien Laurens react to Thomas Tuchel signing a new contract extension with England until 2028.

articles.headline: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
articles.description: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

images.name: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
images.caption: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

articles.headline: Kylian Mbappe's injury 'not good news' for Real Madrid's schedule

article.headline: Italy edge Netherlands to finish third in Nations League
article.description: Italy claimed the consolation bronze medals at the Nations League finals as they edged the Netherlands 3-2 in Sunday's third place playoff tie at the Twente Stadium.
article.linkText: Italy edge Netherlands, finish third in N. League

images.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [1296x729]
images.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
images.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

peers.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
peers.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

peers.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [1296x729]
peers.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
peers.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

peers.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [864x1296]
peers.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
peers.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

peers.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [1296x864]
peers.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
peers.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

peers.name: Italy celeb vs. Netherlands [1296x518]
peers.caption: Italy edge to a 3-2 win over Netherlands at the Twente Stadium on Sunday.
peers.credit: Christopher Lee - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

article.story:
Italy claimed the consolation bronze medals at the Nations League finals, and added to a disappointing week for hosts the Netherlands, as they edged the Dutch 3-2 in Sunday's third place playoff tie at the Twente Stadium.

Federico Dimarco thrashed the ball home at the far post in the sixth minute and Davide Frattesi doubled the score from in front of goal in the 20th minute to give Italy a comfortable halftime lead.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

But the Dutch launched a concerted second half comeback, making three attacking changes at the break, laying siege to Italy's goal and seeing Steven Bergwijn pull a goal back in the 68th minute.

Federico Chiesa restored the two goal advantage at the end of a 73rd minute counterattack but there was an exciting conclusion as Georginio Wijnaldum got a second for the Dutch in the 89th. But even though nine minutes of stoppage time was added on, Italy held out under home pressure for victory.

Italy made a quick start as they put together a sequence of quick passes with Giacomo Raspadori backheeling the ball to Dimarco for a thunderous finish.

The shock of the goal silenced the home fans and there was hardly any noise 14 minutes later when a fortunate rebound fell for Frattesi to score, as he chested the ball down before squeezing it home.

The transformed Dutch side tested Italy goalkeeper Gianluigi Donnarumma in the second half. He made an impressive block to deny Cody Gakpo, who had missed a glaring first-half chance.

Gakpo did contribute, however, as he set up Bergwijn for the home side's first goal, with the substitute showing composure as he dragged the ball from his right to left foot before rifling home.

Five minutes later Chiesa broke from inside his own half down the left flank and squeezed his shot inside the far post as the Dutch were caught out pushing up in search of a second goal.

Wijnaldum's late goal renewed hopes of a comeback but it was not to be for the Dutch, who had high hopes of home success in the tournament but were beaten 4-2 in extra time by Croatia in Wednesday's first semifinal.

Italy lost to a last-minute goal in their semi-final against Spain on Thursday. Croatia and Spain contest the final in Rotterdam later on Sunday.
